cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher power class. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod actor_scope;\\n45\\tpub mod auth;\\n46\\tmod collection_listing;\\n47\\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\\n48\\tpub mod control_plane;\\n49\\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\\n50\\tpub mod Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks. asynchronous `tokio-postgres` crate driven from a dedicated Tokio\\n/// runtime. Because `StorageAdapter` is synchronous, each database call\\n/// blocks the calling thread via `handle.block_on(...)`.\\n///\\n/// Transactions are handled via `BEGIN` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK` statements.\\n/// The adapter creates the required tables on initialization if they do not\\n/// exist.\\npub struct PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    client: tokio_postgres::Client,\\n    handle: tokio::runtime::Handle,\\n    _rt: tokio::runtime::Runtime,\\n    in_tx: bool,\\n}\\n\\nimpl PostgresStorageAdapter {\\n    /// Connect to a PostgreSQL database using a connection string.\\n    ///\\n    /// Example: `\\\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon\\\"`\\n    pub fn connect(params: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, AxonError\u003e {\\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\\n            .worker_threads(2)\\n            .enable_all()\\n            .build()\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let handle = rt.handle().clone();\\n        let (client, connection) = handle\\n            .block_on(tokio_postgres::connect(params, NoTls))\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(format!(\\\"connection failed: {e}\\\")))?;\\n        handle.spawn(async move {\\n            if let Err(e) = connection.await {\\n                tracing::error!(\\\"postgres connection error: {e}\\\");\\n            }\\n        });\\n        let mut adapter = Self {\\n            client,\\n            handle,\\n            _rt: rt,\\n            in_tx: false,\\n        };\\n        adapter.init_schema()?;\\n        Ok(adapter)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Block the current thread on a `tokio_postgres` future, converting any\\n    /// error into `AxonError::Storage`.\\n    ///\\n    /// When called from within a Tokio async task, uses\\n    /// [`tokio::task::block_in_place`] to temporarily un-enter the async\\n    /// context before blocking on the adapter's dedicated runtime.  This\\n    /// prevents the \\\"cannot call `block_on` from within an async context\\\"\\n    /// panic that would otherwise occur when gateway route handlers drive\\n    /// storage calls.\\n    ///\\n    /// Requires the multi-thread Tokio scheduler (used by the production\\n    /// server).  Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\\n    fn block\u003cT\u003e(\\n        \u0026self,\\n        fut: impl Future\u003cOutput = Result\u003cT, tokio_postgres::Error\u003e\u003e,\\n    ) -\u003e Result\u003cT, AxonError\u003e {\\n        match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\\n            Ok(_) =\u003e tokio::task::block_in_place(|| self.handle.block_on(fut)),\\n            Err(_) =\u003e self.handle.block_on(fut),\\n        }\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))\\n    }\\n\\n    fn init_schema(\u0026mut self) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n        self.block(self.client.batch_execute(\\n            \\\"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS databases (\\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS namespaces (\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES databases(name) ON DELETE CASCADE,\\n                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, name)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entities (\\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    id            TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    version       BIGINT NOT NULL,\\n                    data          JSONB NOT NULL,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, id)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schemas (\\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n                    schema_json   JSONB NOT NULL,\\n                    created_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection, version)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collections (\\n                    name TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    schema_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, name)\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS collection_views (\\n                    collection    TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    schema_name   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',\\n                    version       INTEGER NOT NULL,\\n                    view_json     JSONB NOT NULL,\\n                    updated_at_ns BIGINT NOT NULL,\\n                    updated_by    TEXT,\\n                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection),\\n                    FOREIGN KEY (database_name, schema_name, collection)\\n                        REFERENCES collections(database_name, schema_name, name)\\n                        ON DELETE CASCADE\\n                );\\n                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_log (\\n                    id             BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\\n                    timestamp_ns   BIGINT NOT NULL,\\n                    collection     TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    entity_id      TEXT NOT NULL,\\n                    version        BIGINT NOT We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n container.get_host().map_err(|error| {\\n                TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\\n                    \\\"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container host: {error}\\\"\\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! # Verification order\\n//!\\n//! 1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` → `Unauthenticated` if missing.\\n//! 2. Parse + verify HS256 signature → `CredentialInvalid` / `CredentialMalformed`.\\n//! 3. Check `iss` against issuer ID → `CredentialForeignIssuer`.\\n//! 4. Check `exp`/`nbf` with ±30 s skew → `CredentialExpired` / `CredentialNotYetValid`.\\n//! 5. Check `jti` revocation (cache → storage) → `CredentialRevoked`.\\n//! 6. Compare `aud` to URL tenant → `CredentialWrongTenant`.\\n//! 7. Look up `sub` in users table, check suspension → `UserSuspended`.\\n//! 8. Check tenant membership → `NotATenantMember`.\\n//! 9. Walk grants for URL database + HTTP-method-derived op → `DatabaseNotGranted` / `OpNotGranted`.\\n//!\\n//! On success, installs `ResolvedIdentity` into the request extension.\\n//! On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\\n\\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::extract::State;\\nuse axum::middleware::Next;\\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\\nuse http::{HeaderMap, Method, StatusCode};\\nuse jsonwebtoken::{Algorithm, DecodingKey, EncodingKey, Header, Validation, decode, encode};\\nuse serde_json::json;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{AuthError, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, UserId};\\nuse axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── JwtIssuer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\\npub struct JwtIssuer {\\n    secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e,\\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\\n    pub issuer_id: String,\\n}\\n\\nimpl JwtIssuer {\\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec\u003cu8\u003e, issuer_id: String) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n            \u0026Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\\n            claims,\\n            \u0026EncodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret),\\n        )\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\\n    ///\\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\\n    pub fn verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e {\\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\\n\\n        decode::\u003cJwtClaims\u003e(token, \u0026DecodingKey::from_secret(\u0026self.secret), \u0026validation)\\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature =\u003e AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── InMemoryRevocationCache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\\n///\\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\\n#[derive(Default)]\\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    inner: RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\\n    /// Create an empty cache.\\n    pub fn new() -\u003e Self {\\n        Self::default()\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\\n    pub fn contains(\u0026self, jti: \u0026Uuid) -\u003e bool {\\n        self.inner\\n            .read()\\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\\n            .unwrap_or(false)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\\n    pub fn insert(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) {\\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\\n            g.insert(jti);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── AuthPipelineState ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\\n///\\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\\n#[derive(Clone)]\\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\\n    pub issuer: Arc\u003cJwtIssuer\u003e,\\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc\u003cInMemoryRevocationCache\u003e,\\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\\n    pub storage: Arc\u003cMutex\u003cBox\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\u003e\u003e,\\n}\\n\\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\\n    /// Convenience constructor.\\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box\u003cdyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e) -\u003e Self {\\n        Self {\\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Middleware ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 §4 JWT verification order.\\n///\\n/// Install with:\\n/// ```ignore\\n/// Router::new()\\n///     .route(\\\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/…\\\", …)\\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n///         Arc::new(state),\\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\\n///     ))\\n/// ```\\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\\n    State(state): State\u003cArc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e\u003e,\\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\\n    next: Next,\\n) -\u003e Response {\\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\\n\\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(\u0026path) else {\\n        return next.run(request).await;\\n    };\\n\\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\\n    let database = database.to_string();\\n    let method = request.method().clone();\\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\\n\\n    match verify_request(\u0026state, \u0026headers, \u0026method, \u0026tenant, \u0026database) {\\n        Ok(identity) =\u003e {\\n            let mut request = request;\\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\\n            next.run(request).await\\n        }\\n        Err(err) =\u003e AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Core verification logic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nfn verify_request(\\n    state: \u0026AuthPipelineState,\\n    headers: \u0026HeaderMap,\\n    method: \u0026Method,\\n    tenant: \u0026str,\\n    database: \u0026str,\\n) -\u003e Result\u003cResolvedIdentity, AuthError\u003e {\\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\\n\\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\\n\\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs();\\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\\n    // Expired: exp + skew \u003c now  ↔  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) \u003c now {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\\n    }\\n    // Not yet valid: nbf \u003e now + skew  ↔  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\\n    if claims.nbf \u003e now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\\n        .jti\\n        .parse()\\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(\u0026jti_uuid) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n    }\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        if storage\\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\\n        {\\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\\n            drop(storage);\\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\\n    let user_id = UserId::new(\u0026claims.sub);\\n    let user = {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\\n    };\\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\\n    {\\n        let storage = state\\n            .storage\\n            .lock()\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\\n        storage\\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\\n    let granted_db = claims\\n        .grants\\n        .find_database(database)\\n        .ok_or(AuthError::DatabaseNotGranted)?;\\n\\n    let required_op = if method == Method::GET {\\n        Op::Read\\n    } else {\\n        Op::Write\\n    };\\n    if !granted_db.has_op(required_op) {\\n        return Err(AuthError::OpNotGranted);\\n    }\\n\\n    Ok(ResolvedIdentity {\\n        user_id,\\n        tenant_id,\\n        grants: claims.grants,\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/// Extract the `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` value from the `Authorization` header.\\nfn extract_bearer_token(headers: \u0026HeaderMap) -\u003e Result\u003c\u0026str, AuthError\u003e {\\n    let auth_header = headers\\n }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\\n    pub fn issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e {\\n        encode(\\n       jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n                _ =\u003e AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\\n            })\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n//!\\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n//!\\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n//!\\n//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n//!\\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n//!\\n//! # Request flow\\n//!\\n//! ```text\\n//! TCP accept\\n//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 §4).\\n//!\\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\n//!\\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\\n//! 1. No auth header → 401 unauthenticated\\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants → 200\\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant\\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 database_not_granted\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\nconst ISSUER: \u0026str = \\\"integration-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated ──────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test   .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", bearer(\u0026token))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud → 403 credential_wrong_tenant ─────────────────\\n\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\\n    let state = assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"credential_wrong_tenant\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 4: Grants for different database → 403 directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. data.text: I’ve confirmed `failclass` already encodes the non-escalating classes. The remaining work is the worker’s retry planner: I’m now tracing where it computes requested min power or profile after an attempt so I can block escalation for structural/system outcomes there.

data.text: The review path has its own min-power escalation logic, so I’m checking whether this bead’s bug actually lives there rather than in the implementer loop. If the escalation decision is shared, I’ll patch the shared classifier boundary and keep the review-specific behavior unchanged.

I hit a bad worktree path on the last probe, so I’m rerunning against the actual execution directory. I’m specifically looking for the reviewer min-power bump and the test harnesses that already encode the escalation contract.

data.text: I’ve narrowed it to three worker-side branches: no-changes smart retry, repair-cycle exhaustion, and provider-connectivity/no-viable-provider handling. The likely fix is to gate the `EscalationNextFloor` consult behind the typed failure class, then prove the excluded classes stay on same-tier or operator-attention paths.

data.text: The pre-claim side is clearly classifying `system_unready` as fail-open, so the remaining question is where that signal leaks into escalation state. I’m reading the readiness classifier and the loop’s pre-claim warning accumulator next; if escalation is still happening, it will be because the loop is treating those warnings as generic retry evidence instead of operator-attention only.

The pre-claim warnings themselves already end in operator-attention, so I’m now tracing the actual routing floor state that survives into the next attempt. The fix likely belongs where the worker writes or reads the next-floor hint from bead metadata, because that’s the only place a non-semantic outcome can accidentally become a stronger requested model.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the worker already isolates pre-claim warnings and provider-connectivity retries. The likely remaining bug is the no-changes adjudication path, where some “bad attempt” outcomes still get a higher next floor even when the failure wasn’t semantic. I’m reading that classifier now so I can separate acceptance/review failures from infrastructure or bookkeeping noise.

data.text: I’ve got enough to make a narrow change: the current no-changes fallback unconditionally consults the next-floor callback, which is exactly the kind of “non-semantic” path this bead wants to suppress. I’m now reading the existing no-changes loop tests so I can extend them instead of inventing a new contract.

data.text: I found the real fault line: the generic escalation state machine still treats `land_conflict` as escalation-worthy, even though the normative failure table says merge/land coordination is not a tier-raising signal. That’s the shared worker path that can bump power/profile on a non-semantic failure, so I’m checking its call sites and the current tests before I patch it.

The unjustified and lifecycle-status paths are the ones that should stop burning min-power. I’m checking the existing loop tests around that split so the patch preserves the verified path and cuts only the non-semantic ones.

data.text: I’m making the code change now. The core of it is: keep the semantic verification-failure path eligible for a higher floor, but stop auto-bumping min power for open/unjustified/no-evidence retries, provider connectivity, and land coordination. I’m also updating the tests to match that narrower contract.

I’m updating the no-changes tests to match the narrower contract too. The old suite treated unjustified/no-evidence retries as min-power escalation cases; that is exactly what this bead is removing, so those expectations need to flip while the verified-failure case stays escalatable.

I’ve got the behavior change in place. I’m doing a quick cleanup pass on the comments and then I’ll run the targeted test slice first, because the two most likely regressions are the no-changes expectations and the escalatable-status alignment.
I’ve patched the escalation path so only semantic failure paths can consume min-power/profile escalation. Next I’m validating the changed tests and any compile fallout before I stage anything.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-3a6f1ad6">     <title>agent: classify land coordination failures as reconciliation</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Landing failures that are really ref-lock, already-landed, or staged-evidence coordination issues still look like ordinary implementation failures to the worker. That causes fresh implementation reruns and the wrong retry lane instead of reconciliation or operator attention.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:292-312` wraps every `LandingAdvancer` error as a generic `land advancer` failure, which loses whether the candidate was already landed, whether `refs/heads/main` could not be locked, or whether the landing worktree was dirty. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_report_test.go:57-62` only proves the current detail string records land failure; it does not distinguish the coordination class from implementation failure.

PROPOSED FIX
- Split land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures into explicit retry actions: reconcile, retry-land, or operator-attention.
- If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and continue without rerunning implementation.
- If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, preserve or retry the land/evidence step according to local policy rather than escalating the model.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating, decomposition detection, or power escalation policy in this child.
- Do not reimplement low-level tracker/index contention fixes owned by sibling work.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.
2. The regression shows the worker preserves or reconciles land state instead of rerunning implementation when the land result is already effectively landed or blocked by dirty staged evidence.

4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.

The worker can treat a successful cycle as closeable even when `ReviewResult` is empty or the reviewer is nil without an explicit skip reason. That matches the Niflheim success records where `cycle_trace.review_result={}` and nil-reviewer paths still reached close.

Nothing currently rejects a non-skipped empty review result before close.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guard in the post-review or success path that treats an empty non-skipped `ReviewResult` as a review error or operator-attention event.
- Require an explicit durable skip reason for `Reviewer: nil` or `NoReview` before close.
- Add regression coverage for empty review close-blocking and explicit skip behavior.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change land coordination, pre-claim intake, or decomposition handling in this child.
- Do not alter the review model itself beyond the gating invariants needed for these regressions.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.

The worker still treats non-semantic retry classes, including repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings, as if they were the same as acceptance failures. That can raise the requested min power or profile even though a stronger model cannot fix the underlying problem.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` already defines the normative boundary: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` escalates, while dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict outcomes do not. In the worker loop, however, the retry/escalation decision is not consistently reduced to that typed table before min-power/profile selection, so system warnings and structural failures can still drift into the escalation path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Wire the retry classifier into the worker path that chooses the next requested min power or profile.
- Treat repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings as operator attention or same-tier retry, not escalation.
- Keep decomposed-parent outcomes, land coordination failures, and dirty generated DDx evidence out of the min-power escalation path.
- Allow only semantic acceptance/review failures to raise min power/profile.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating or land reconciliation mechanics in this child.
- Do not alter the low-level failure-class enum unless the worker path needs the typed mapping.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile.
